task_completion.description: Run the initial test suite to observe the failing pipeline tests.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing the execution of a test runner (like pytest) in the /home/ga/workspace/smart_meter_pipeline/ directory, displaying failing tests (red text, tracebacks, or 'FAILED' indicators).

task_completion.description: Modify the pipeline code to fix data parsing and imputation issues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor open to the pipeline source code showing modifications to pandas date parsing (e.g., handling European date formats using dayfirst=True or specific format strings) and missing data imputation (e.g., fillna, interpolate).

task_completion.description: Modify the pipeline code to fix time-series aggregation and tariff boundary logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing updates to pandas time-series aggregation (e.g., resample, groupby) or logic evaluating tariff boundaries to prevent customer overbilling.

task_completion.description: Modify the pipeline code to fix the adaptive anomaly detection logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing updates to anomaly detection algorithms, such as adjusting rolling means, standard deviations, or thresholds to account for winter heating drift and reduce false positives.

task_completion.description: Run the test suite to verify all pipeline issues are resolved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing the execution of the test runner with all tests passing successfully (green text, 'OK', or '100% passed').

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not modify the test files to trivially pass the assertions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code editor should not show modifications to the files inside the tests/ directory that comment out assertions or change expected values.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually executed the test suite rather than faking the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should display genuine test runner output (e.g., pytest collection, execution times, and summary) rather than a simple printed string or echo command.

integrity.description: Verify the agent implemented generalized pandas logic rather than hardcoding answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The pipeline code should contain standard pandas operations (e.g., pd.to_datetime, df.resample) rather than hardcoded arrays, dictionaries, or if-statements designed to bypass specific test cases.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires resolving 5 specific pandas-based pipeline bugs related to: 1) data parsing (handling European date formats), 2) data imputation, 3) time-series aggregation, 4) tariff boundaries, and 5) adaptive anomaly detection (accounting for winter heating drift). The agent must achieve a passing score of at least 60 on the test suite.
